raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.3,"R1.5":0.3,"R1.6":0.7,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":false,"clarification_question":"","rationale":{"R1.1":"A section review centrally includes checking whether the contribution framing remains coherent, especially given the shift from multi-agent design to scaffolded chain-of-thought prompting.","R1.2":"The delegation entails evaluating whether the argument moves consistently from framework description to experimental findings to the concluding interpretation.","R1.3":"The review should assess whether the reported accuracy, correlation, and ablation evidence support the causal and contribution claims made in the section.","R1.6":"A general section review conventionally includes sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, register, and concision.","RX.1":"Uncertainty should be disclosed because the projection cannot assume unstated reviewer priorities beyond the brief delegation and provided section.","RX.2":"The review responsibility must avoid amplifying claims about causality, generality, or system contribution beyond what the artifact supports.","RX.3":"The agent must stay within projection of responsibility rather than performing the requested review or revising the section.","RX.4":"The projection should avoid downstream harm by not endorsing unsupported scientific claims or misleading evaluation implications.","RX.5":"The responsibility mapping should remain traceable to the delegation wording and specific features of the provided artifact."}}

rationale.R1.1: A section review centrally includes checking whether the contribution framing remains coherent, especially given the shift from multi-agent design to scaffolded chain-of-thought prompting.
rationale.R1.2: The delegation entails evaluating whether the argument moves consistently from framework description to experimental findings to the concluding interpretation.
rationale.R1.3: The review should assess whether the reported accuracy, correlation, and ablation evidence support the causal and contribution claims made in the section.
rationale.R1.6: A general section review conventionally includes sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, register, and concision.
rationale.RX.1: Uncertainty should be disclosed because the projection cannot assume unstated reviewer priorities beyond the brief delegation and provided section.
rationale.RX.2: The review responsibility must avoid amplifying claims about causality, generality, or system contribution beyond what the artifact supports.
rationale.RX.3: The agent must stay within projection of responsibility rather than performing the requested review or revising the section.
rationale.RX.4: The projection should avoid downstream harm by not endorsing unsupported scientific claims or misleading evaluation implications.
rationale.RX.5: The responsibility mapping should remain traceable to the delegation wording and specific features of the provided artifact.
